source.title: Fatal Naphtha Release and Fire at BP-Husky Toledo Refinery

context.operator: BP Products North America Inc. (now Ohio Refining Company LLC)

event.top_event: Loss of Containment - Naphtha Release and Fire
event.incident_type: Flash Fire from Vapor Cloud Ignition
event.summary: On September 20, 2022, at approximately 6:46 p.m., a vapor cloud ignited causing a flash fire at the BP-Husky Toledo Refinery in Oregon, Ohio. Two BP employees (brothers Ben and Max Morrissey) were fatally injured when they released approximately 23,000 pounds of flammable liquid naphtha from a pressurized Fuel Gas Mix Drum to the ground. The drum, which normally contained only vapor (fuel gas), had filled with liquid naphtha when an upstream Crude 1 Tower overflowed naphtha into a vapor bypass line. The incident resulted in approximately $597 million in property damage and is the largest fatal incident at a BP-operated petroleum refinery since the 2005 Texas City incident.
recommendations: Revise safeguards in process hazard analyses to establish effective preventive safeguards using engineered controls that do not rely solely on human intervention

Revise Abnormal Situation Management policy to incorporate CCPS guidance and include procedures for unplanned crude slate changes, continued operation with units shut down, and emergency pressure-relief valve opening
Develop and implement Stop Work Authority policy with clear procedures and regular training for employees to stop unsafe work during abnormal situations
Revise Toledo Alarm Philosophy to incorporate EEMUA guidance for alarm rate following upset and improve alarm performance to reduce alarm flood duration and peak rate
API should develop guidance on Fuel Gas Mix Drum design, sizing, instrumentation, and preventive safeguards
API should develop publication addressing prevention of pressure vessel overflow with guidance on instrumentation, process hazard analysis, safeguards, and training
ISA should revise ANSI/ISA 18.2-2016 to include performance targets for short-term alarm flood analysis

hazards.name: Flammable Liquid Naphtha in Pressurized Vessel
Vapor Cloud Formation from Liquid Release
Ignition Source Proximity to Vapor Cloud
Operator Cognitive Overload from Alarm Flood

threats.name: Crude 1 Tower Overflow into Vapor Bypass Line
Absorber Stripper Tower Overflow to Fuel Gas Mix Drum
Fuel Gas Mix Drum High Level Condition
Naphtha Hydrotreater Unit Shutdown During Operation
Operator Decision to Drain Pressurized Vessel Manually
Alarm Flood Preventing Effective Operator Response

consequences.name: Property Damage and Loss of Use

controls.name: Crude 1 Tower Level Control System
controls.lod_basis: Basic process control system for level management

supporting_text: a board operator opened a closed valve sending liquid naphtha to the tower operating in a vapor-only mode
Crude 1 Tower Instability
Crude 1 Overhead Accumulator Drum High Level

controls.name: Absorber Stripper Tower Overflow Prevention
controls.barrier_role: Prevent overflow of Absorber Stripper Tower to Fuel Gas Mix Drum
controls.lod_basis: Process design safeguard identified in PHA

supporting_text: the refinery did not have sufficient safeguards to prevent the initiating event
In some cases, the BP Toledo Refinery relied on human intervention to respond to process upsets and deviations
Absorber Stripper Tower Overflow

controls.name: Fuel Gas Mix Drum Level Instrumentation
controls.barrier_role: Detect high liquid level in Fuel Gas Mix Drum
controls.lod_basis: Instrumentation for early detection of abnormal condition

supporting_text: the refinery did not adequately consider potential hazards that could exist if the drum contained high levels of flammable liquid
the refinery did not have procedures, written instructions, or documented corrective actions for operators to respond to or troubleshoot a high liquid level in the Fuel Gas Mix Drum

controls.name: Furnace Safety Instrumented Systems
controls.barrier_role: Prevent liquid overflow to fuel gas system via furnace protection
controls.lod_basis: Safety instrumented function for furnace protection

supporting_text: Furnace safety instrumented systems and emergency pressure-relief valves were also identified as safeguards in the BP Toledo Refinery's PHAs for overflow of the Absorber Stripper Tower to the Fuel Gas Mix Drum, but neither were effective in preventing liquid overflow to the fuel gas system

controls.name: Emergency Pressure-Relief Valves
controls.barrier_role: Relieve pressure and prevent vessel rupture during overflow
controls.lod_basis: Pressure relief safeguard identified in PHA

supporting_text: emergency pressure-relief valves were also identified as safeguards in the BP Toledo Refinery's PHAs for overflow of the Absorber Stripper Tower to the Fuel Gas Mix Drum, but neither were effective in preventing liquid overflow to the fuel gas system
an emergency pressure-relief valve opening

controls.name: Abnormal Situation Management Policy
controls.barrier_role: Manage process upsets and prevent escalation to incident
controls.lod_basis: Operational procedure for abnormal condition management

supporting_text: the refinery's ineffective policies, procedures, and practices to avoid and control abnormal situations
In the 24 hours leading up to the incident, the BP Toledo Refinery experienced a number of abnormal situations across several units, escalating to overfilling the Fuel Gas Mix Drum
the refinery did not adequately consider potential hazards that could exist if the drum contained high levels of flammable liquid

controls.name: Alarm System Management
controls.barrier_role: Alert operators to abnormal conditions and enable timely response

supporting_text: Board operators at the BP Toledo Refinery were receiving far more than 10 alarms in 10 minutes on average, a situation in which more alarms were annunciating than a human can effectively respond to, for nearly 12 hours preceding the incident
Between 6:50 a.m. and 6:49 p.m. September 20, 2022, a total of 3,712 alarms were recorded
Continued operation in an alarm flood state contributed to the incident by causing delays and errors in responding to critical alarms

controls.name: Stop Work Authority Policy
controls.barrier_role: Empower employees to halt unsafe operations during abnormal situations
controls.lod_basis: Operational safety procedure for employee authority

supporting_text: the refinery's failure to implement a shutdown or hot circulation through the use of Stop Work Authority or otherwise

While draining the overflowing vessel as fast as they could pursuant to the board operator's directive communicated via radio, the BP employees opened the vessel and released liquid naphtha to the ground

controls.name: Process Hazard Analysis for Overflow Scenarios
controls.barrier_role: Identify and document safeguards for overflow prevention
controls.lod_basis: OSHA PSM requirement for hazard identification

supporting_text: Although the BP Toledo Refinery conducted Hazard and Operability Studies, a process hazard analysis (PHA) methodology, to assess the risk of liquid overflow events and identify safeguards, the refinery did not have sufficient safeguards to prevent the initiating event
the refinery did not adequately consider potential hazards that could exist if the drum contained high levels of flammable liquid

controls.name: Operator Training on Overflow Response
controls.barrier_role: Ensure operators understand procedures for responding to high liquid levels

supporting_text: the refinery did not have procedures, written instructions, or documented corrective actions for operators to respond to or troubleshoot a high liquid level in the Fuel Gas Mix Drum during either normal operations or process upsets if liquid entered the drum

controls.name: Incident Learning and Knowledge Transfer
controls.barrier_role: Apply lessons from previous incidents to prevent recurrence
controls.lod_basis: Process safety management requirement for continuous improvement

supporting_text: the refinery's failure to learn from previous incidents

Catastrophic incident warning signs existed prior to the September 20, 2022, incident at the BP Toledo Refinery, and had BP effectively recognized and acted upon the warning signs following a 2019 incident, the company could have provided more effective safeguards
similarities between the overflow events of the BP Toledo Refinery incident and the findings from the fatal explosions in 2005 at the BP refinery in Texas City, Texas

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
